findings.title: appSchemaURL inconsistency vs sibling URLs in DirectConfig
findings.summary: Four of the five DirectConfig URL constants (upstreamDonateURL, sponsorURL, faqURL, githubURL) migrated to the new URL(staticString:) initializer and are now non-optional URL. appSchemaURL remains URL? via URL(string: "dosbts://"). A future reader will wonder why this one constant opts out of the sweep. There is no documented reason — URL(staticString: "dosbts://") would construct fine at compile time since any valid scheme URL parses. The Optional leaks into AppleCalendarExport.swift (lines 114, 155, 185) where it is compared to EKEvent.url (Optional URL); the comparison works today, but the asymmetry is accidental complexity.
findings.recommendation: Either migrate appSchemaURL to URL(staticString: "dosbts://") for consistency with the sweep, or add a one-line comment explaining why it must stay optional (e.g. "left optional in case schema changes at runtime" — though nothing in the codebase suggests that). Full consistency is cheaper than a comment.

findings.title: Orphan /* (No Comment) */ markers left behind in all 19 locale files
findings.summary: The three orphan key removals (App donate, App facebook group, App translation) deleted only the key=value line in each locale file, leaving the associated /* (No Comment) */ comment markers in place. Each of the 19 Localizable.strings now has 3 stranded comment blocks followed by blank lines — perfectly consistent across locales (good), but cosmetically dead text that serves no purpose. Total: ~57 orphan markers.
findings.recommendation: Delete the now-orphaned /* (No Comment) */ markers as a one-line sed sweep per locale. Low urgency since the files are generated/edited rarely, but the next person who grep-audits Localizable.strings will have to filter this noise.

findings.title: Stale 'iOS 15 sibling sheet collision' code comment in OverviewView
findings.summary: The MARK comment reads `// MARK: - Active Sheet Enum (prevents iOS 15 sibling sheet collision)`. The ActiveSheet pattern is still correct (SwiftUI nested sheets are unreliable on all iOS versions — CLAUDE.md explicitly states this), but the iOS-15-specific justification is now historical since the deployment target is iOS 26.0. A reader encountering this comment today will either (a) be confused about why iOS 15 is referenced, or (b) conclude the pattern is no longer needed. Neither is correct.
findings.recommendation: Reword to something like `// MARK: - Active Sheet Enum (prevents SwiftUI sibling-sheet collisions — see CLAUDE.md)`. Preserves architectural intent without anchoring to a dropped OS version.

findings.title: CLAUDE.md still contains 'iOS 15' archaeological references after the sweep this PR documents
findings.summary: Line 43 says `Existing #available guards are now dead branches and can be cleaned up incrementally.` This PR *is* that cleanup — post-merge, no iOS 15/16/17 #available guards remain in App/, Library/, or Widgets/ (verified via grep). The sentence is now untrue: nothing further to incrementally clean. Line 48 justifies the ActiveSheet enum with an iOS-15-specific reason that no longer applies (same as maint-3). Line 45 is fine (explicitly says 'This applies to all iOS versions, not just iOS 15').
findings.recommendation: Update line 43 to state the cleanup is complete (e.g. `Legacy #available guards were swept in DMNC-777; none remain`) and line 48 to match whatever wording is chosen for the OverviewView MARK comment. Low urgency — CLAUDE.md is a living doc — but worth touching now since this PR is the closing chapter on iOS-version guards.

residual_risks: URL(staticString:) uses string interpolation ("\(staticString)") which defeats some of the compile-time StaticString benefit — but per PR intent this was the blessed DMNC-776 design choice. Not flagged per review scope.

preconditionFailure in URL(staticString:) means a typo in a URL literal crashes the app at first use rather than at compile time. For the 5 hardcoded URLs in this PR the risk is effectively zero (all obviously-valid HTTPS URLs), but the pattern has no test coverage. Low concern in practice.
The new Library/Extensions/URL.swift auto-compiles into both DOSBTSApp and DOSBTSWidget targets (fileSystemSynchronized). This is correct and matches sibling extensions — no duplication risk.
testing_gaps: No unit test exercises URL(staticString:) — the preconditionFailure path is unreachable in a well-formed call site but still untested. A single #expect over a known-good URL would prevent accidental future regressions in the extension file (e.g. someone changing the initializer signature).

No snapshot or build-verification test asserts that localized strings referenced in code all exist in the .strings files. Today's sweep is safe because 'App donate'/'App facebook group'/'App translation' were confirmed orphaned via grep, but the codebase has no mechanism to prevent a future string-code drift from going unnoticed.
